name: Tan Teck Long

work_experience.title: Various senior roles (nearly 30 years)
work_experience.description: Held multiple senior positions at DBS Bank over close to 30 years, including Group Chief Risk Officer, Head of Corporate Banking, COO of Institutional Banking, Head of Institutional Banking Group in China (2011-2016), Head of DBS' large corporate clients, and Group Head of Special Assets Management.

work_experience.title: Head of Global Wholesale Banking
work_experience.description: Responsible for all banking relationships with SMEs, large corporates and financial institutions. Oversaw cash management, trade, transaction banking, and investment banking. The wholesale banking business made up about 38% of OCBC's overall profit before tax in 2024.

work_experience.title: Deputy CEO
work_experience.description: Appointed Deputy CEO to facilitate transition before becoming Group CEO.

work_experience.title: Group Chief Executive Officer
work_experience.description: Succeeded Helen Wong as Group CEO of OCBC Bank, one of Southeast Asia's largest financial institutions. Selected after a rigorous global search by the board.

person_relationships.description: Tan Teck Long succeeded Helen Wong as OCBC Group CEO on 1 January 2026 after a six-month transition period.

major_achievements.achievement: Appointed Group CEO of OCBC Bank, unanimously selected by the board after a rigorous global search.
As Head of Global Wholesale Banking, achieved 35% revenue growth and more than 50% customer growth from surging Greater China-Asean flows.
The wholesale banking division under his leadership contributed approximately 38% of OCBC's overall profit before tax.

biography_summary: Tan Teck Long (born circa 1970) is a Singaporean banker who has served as Group Chief Executive Officer of OCBC Bank since January 2026, succeeding Helen Wong. A veteran with over 30 years of banking experience, he spent nearly three decades at DBS Bank where he held senior positions including Group Chief Risk Officer, Head of Corporate Banking, and Head of Institutional Banking Group in China. He joined OCBC in March 2022 as Head of Global Wholesale Banking, overseeing relationships with SMEs, large corporates and financial institutions globally. Under his leadership, the wholesale banking division achieved strong revenue growth, particularly from Greater China-Asean flows. A Mandarin speaker with deep knowledge of the China market, he also served as director on the boards of OCBC-affiliated Bank of Ningbo and Maxwealth Fund Management Company in China. He was unanimously selected as CEO by the OCBC board after a rigorous global search.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with data from Business Times, OCBC official site, DBS, CNA, and Fintech News. Full career timeline spanning DBS and OCBC, education, biography, achievements, and relationships added.
